raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, framing, and contribution claim in the research draft.","R1.2":"Evaluating what one thinks of the passage centrally includes checking whether the argument from problem statement to metric choice to interpretation holds together.","R1.3":"The artifact makes performance and liveness claims tied to reported benchmark results, so claim-support alignment is an expected responsibility.","R1.4":"The passage explicitly asserts a first standardization contribution, making novelty assessment central to the projected responsibility.","R1.6":"A general reaction to a draft normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and flow.","R1.7":"The artifact relies on named prior work and attribution claims, so citation and scholarship responsibility is central.","RX.1":"Any assessment should disclose uncertainty where the excerpt lacks full paper context, table details, or bibliography access.","RX.2":"The projection must guard against endorsing broad liveness, performance, or novelty claims beyond what the artifact supports.","RX.3":"The responsibility remains limited to evaluating the draft excerpt rather than executing edits or external verification.","RX.4":"The assessment should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsafe downstream reliance on unsupported benchmarking conclusions.","RX.5":"Judgments should be traceable to specific statements in the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, framing, and contribution claim in the research draft.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the passage centrally includes checking whether the argument from problem statement to metric choice to interpretation holds together.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes performance and liveness claims tied to reported benchmark results, so claim-support alignment is an expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The passage explicitly asserts a first standardization contribution, making novelty assessment central to the projected responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A general reaction to a draft normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and flow.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact relies on named prior work and attribution claims, so citation and scholarship responsibility is central.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment should disclose uncertainty where the excerpt lacks full paper context, table details, or bibliography access.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must guard against endorsing broad liveness, performance, or novelty claims beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility remains limited to evaluating the draft excerpt rather than executing edits or external verification.
rationale.RX.4: The assessment should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsafe downstream reliance on unsupported benchmarking conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: Judgments should be traceable to specific statements in the provided artifact.
